author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! śuna śuna tu baḍi goṅāra
chāḍiyā satera saṅga, asat⋅saṅge sadā raṅga,
pariṇāma nā kara vicāra

kāmādira vaśa ha’ye, sadā phira matta ha’ye,
jāna’ tomā akṣaya amara
daṇḍa kartā āche jei, daṇḍe daṇḍe likhe sei,
tileke bhāṅgibe garva tora

khara⋅prāya baha bhāra, jebā kanyā⋅putra⋅dāra,
pāla’ jā’re āpanā jāniyā
jabe kāla bāndhi’ labe, e deha paḍiyā ra’be,
dekhi’ mukha rahibe phiriyā

kariyā bāhira⋅bāṭī, gṛhe dibe chaḍā⋅jhāṭī,
snāna kare pavitra lāgiyā
kaha dekhi kebā chila, kāhāra ādara kaila,
ebe kene phele poḍāiyā

kahe premānanda cita, jadi cāha nija hita,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha śvāsa śvāsa
kṛṣṇa jagatera kartā, kṛṣṇa tina⋅lokatrātā,
bhaji’ kṛṣṇa kāṭa karmaphā̐sa
en_translation:
O my mind! Listen, you are very stubborn. Leaving the company of the saintly and always enjoying bad company, you do not consider the consequences.

Becoming subject to lust and other desires, always wandering about intoxicated, you think yourself to be immortal. But the Supreme Judge records your every moment, and in an instant, your pride will be shattered.

Like a donkey, you carry heavy burdens—your family and children—whom you nurture while thinking they are your own. When Time eventually binds you, this body will remain fallen, and everyone's face will turn away from it.

They will take your body outside and sweep the house, then bathe to become purified. Tell me, who was that body really? Who was it that they once cherished? Why do they now throw it away to be burnt?

Premānanda says: "If you desire your own welfare, chant 'Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!' with every breath. Lord Kṛṣṇa is the Master of the universe and the deliverer of the three worlds. By worshipping Him, cut the noose of your past actions."
